title: PushStatus
description: The durable push outbox state, recovered from SQLite: intents still awaiting approval, intents approved but not yet executed, failed push diagnostics, and completed receipts.

PushErrorCode.description: A machine-readable code for a refused push operation, serialized as the shared `opensks.git-error.v1` contract.

oneOf.description: The supplied `--effect-digest` did not match the intent's current digest (the oid or ref moved). No usable approval is recorded; execution is refused.
No still-valid approval exists for the intent; execution is refused.
The ref is protected and no approval acknowledged it (`--ack-protected`).
The remote push itself failed (e.g. an unreachable remote). The local commit and the pending intent are preserved for retry.
The referenced intent does not exist in the durable store.

PushFailureDiagnostic.description: A durable diagnostic for a remote push attempt that failed after approval. It deliberately stores only the reviewed push effect and a machine-readable code, never the raw remote URL, stderr, credentials, or argv.

PushIntent.description: A persisted push intent: the redacted remote, the ref, the local oid to be pushed, the remote's currently-observed oid (or `None`), and the `effect_digest` that binds them. Emitted by `push-enqueue` and recovered from the durable store by `push-status`.

effect_digest.description: Stable hash over {redacted remote url, ref, local_oid, remote_expected_oid}.

protected.description: True when `ref` is a protected branch (`main`/`master`/…); execution then requires an approval recorded with `--ack-protected`.

remote_expected_oid.description: The remote's observed oid for `ref` at enqueue time, or `None` when the ref does not yet exist on the remote (a create).

remote_url_redacted.description: Credential-redacted remote URL; never carries userinfo.

PushReceipt.description: The receipt for an executed push. `already_done` is true when a prior completed receipt with the same idempotency key was reused (the push ran at most once).
